versions.version_description: The other 2 are not actually all the right chords or lyrics. Ver. 1 is very basic, but incomplete (as is Ver. 2), and Ver. 2 tried to get most of the right chords, but put them in wrong places.

Complete version with chorded link chords,palm mute intro,verse 1 and also links ,you tube https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eFjjO_lhf9c .Song from 1985 from the album Reckless ,strangeley although a worldwide hit ,only reached no 42 in the UK charts.

tuning: E A D G B E
content:
Summer Of Sixty Nine - Bryan Adams

[Intro]
[ch]D[/ch]  [ch]A[/ch]

[Verse 1]
[tab][ch]D[/ch]                              [ch]A[/ch]
  I got my first real six-string, bought it at the five-and-dime[/tab]
[tab][ch]D[/ch]                           [ch]A[/ch]
  Played 'til my fingers bled, it was the summer of '69[/tab]

[Chorus 1]
[tab][ch]D[/ch]                            [ch]A[/ch]
  Me and some guys from school, had a band and we tried real hard[/tab]
[tab][ch]D[/ch]                               [ch]A[/ch]
  Jimmy quit and Jody got married, I shoulda known we'd never get far[/tab]

[tab][ch]Bm[/ch]                  [ch]A[/ch]          [ch]D[/ch]                             [ch]G[/ch]
  Oh, when I look back now, that summer seemed to last forever[/tab]
[tab][ch]Bm[/ch]                 [ch]A[/ch]           [ch]D[/ch]                    [ch]G[/ch]
   And if I had the choice, yeah I'd always wanna be there[/tab]
[tab][ch]Bm[/ch]                      [ch]A[/ch]                  [ch]D/A[/ch]     [ch]D[/ch]
  Those were the best days of my life[/tab]

[Verse 2]
[tab][ch]A/D[/ch] - [ch]D[/ch]                            [ch]D/A[/ch] - [ch]A[/ch]
       Ain't no use in complainin',  when you got a job to do[/tab]
[tab][ch]A/D[/ch] - [ch]D[/ch]                                        [ch]D/A[/ch] [ch]A[/ch]
       Spent my evenin's down at the drive-in, and that's when I met you[/tab]

[Chorus 2]
[tab][ch]Bm[/ch]                        [ch]A[/ch]                 [ch]D[/ch]                     [ch]G[/ch]
  Standin' on your mama's porch, you told me that you'd wait forever[/tab]
[tab][ch]Bm[/ch]                        [ch]A[/ch]        [ch]D[/ch]                   [ch]G[/ch]
  Oh, and when you held my hand, I knew that it was now or never[/tab]
[tab][ch]Bm[/ch]                      [ch]A[/ch]             [ch]D[/ch]
  Those were the best days of my life[/tab]
[tab][ch]D[/ch]                                     [ch]A[/ch]       [ch]D/A[/ch]    [ch]D[/ch]
 (Oh yeah)     Back in the summer of 69[/tab]

[Bridge]
[tab][ch]F[/ch]               [ch]Bb[/ch]                  [ch]C[/ch]
   Man we were killin' time, we were young and restless[/tab]
[tab][ch]Bb/D[/ch]      [ch]C/E[/ch]    [ch]F[/ch]
We needed to unwind[/tab]
[tab]         [ch]Bb[/ch]                [ch]C[/ch]
I guess nothin' can last forever, forever no[/tab]

[Verse 3]
[ch]D[/ch] [ch]G/D[/ch] [ch]D[/ch] | [ch]A[/ch] [ch]D/A[/ch] [ch]A[/ch] (x2)

[tab][ch]A/D[/ch] - [ch]D[/ch]                                 [ch]D/A[/ch] - [ch]A[/ch]
      And now the times are changin', look at everything that's come and gone[/tab]
[tab][ch]A/D[/ch] - [ch]D[/ch]                                           [ch]D/A[/ch] - [ch]A[/ch]
      Somethimes when I play that old 6-string, I think about you, wonder what went wrong[/tab]

[Chorus 3]
[tab][ch]Bm[/ch]                        [ch]A[/ch]         [ch]D[/ch]                     [ch]G[/ch]
  Standin' on your mama's porch, you told me that it'd last forever[/tab]
[tab][ch]Bm[/ch]                        [ch]A[/ch]         [ch]D[/ch]                 [ch]G[/ch]
  Oh, and when you held my hand, I knew that it was now or never[/tab]
[tab][ch]Bm[/ch]                      [ch]A[/ch]             [ch]D[/ch]
  Those were the best days of my life[/tab]

[Outro]
[tab][ch]D[/ch]                            [ch]A[/ch]                     [ch]D[/ch] - [ch]A[/ch]
     (Oh yeah)           It was the summer of 69[/tab]

(repeat all the way to the end)
